items.title: Gucci Shifts Into High Gear With F1 Team Sponsorship, New Racing Platform
items.summary: Gucci becomes Alpine's title partner from the 2027 F1 season in a three-year deal reported near $150 million, creating 'Gucci Racing' as a new experiential platform and making Gucci the first luxury house to headline an F1 team. Coverage frames former Alpine CEO and new Kering chief Luca de Meo as a key architect of the deal.

items.title: Incoming CEO Luca de Meo Pledges to Get Kering's 'Mojo Back'
items.summary: Kering shareholders approved Luca de Meo's appointment as CEO with a 98.97% vote. De Meo said the group needs to 'get some mojo back with the market' and cited prior turnaround experience; the appointment is read by US financial press as a signal event for Gucci's ongoing recovery narrative.

items.title: Gucci Official X Account Posts Nettspend Pop-Up at Gucci Wooster NYC
items.summary: Gucci's brand account posted about rapper Nettspend appearing at the Gucci Wooster store in SoHo to celebrate 'Generation Gucci' and his album 'early life crisis.' Fan and press accounts (X, Instagram, Threads) amplified the event same-day, framed as evidence of Demna-era Gucci courting hip-hop/streetwear youth culture.

items.title: Nettspend's Gucci Pop-Up Proved the Continuing Convergence of High Fashion and Hip Hop
items.summary: Hypebeast recaps the Wooster Street pop-up marking Nettspend's continued Gucci partnership since Demna's arrival, describing crowds gathering on the street and merchandise/signing activity. Framed as a case study in Gucci's youth-culture courtship strategy under the new creative direction.

items.title: Fan Instagram Post: 'Nettspend Just Hosted a Pop Up w Gucci in NYC'
items.summary: Fan/community Instagram account documents crowd scenes from the Nettspend x Gucci SoHo pop-up, part of organic (non-brand-owned) amplification of the event circulating same day among streetwear and hip-hop fan accounts.

items.title: PurseForum Thread: 'Gucci Price Increase' Ongoing Discussion
items.summary: Active PurseForum thread where members compare recent Gucci US retail price hikes against perceived quality, with several posters saying prices have outpaced craftsmanship improvements and some saying they are shifting spend to Bottega Veneta or Saint Laurent instead.

items.title: Consumer Reviews Cite Durability and Repair-Service Complaints
items.summary: Recent US consumer reviews describe hardware tarnishing, GG Marmont chain wear and paint chipping within months of purchase, faulty earring backings, and frustration that Gucci charges for repairs on what customers view as manufacturing defects -- feeding a recurring 'price versus quality' critique.

items.title: BBB New York Complaint Log Shows Repeat Service Grievances at Flagship Stores
items.summary: Complaint filings against Gucci's New York operations describe a bag sold as new arriving with visible wear/stains, store staff disputing 'final sale' terms, and long checkout/service wait times attributed to understaffing -- a steady undercurrent of NA retail-experience criticism distinct from the fashion-press narrative.

items.title: BoF Organisation Page Tracks Gucci Under De Meo/Demna Turnaround Watch
items.summary: BoF's continuously updated Gucci tracker frames the brand's current storyline around the de Meo CEO transition, Demna's creative reset, and whether early 2026 stabilization signs (smaller comp declines, F1/Alpine deal, beauty license resolution) will hold into H2 amid persistent wholesale softness noted by Bernstein and UBS.

items.title: Resale Data: Trend-Driven Gucci Bags Losing More Than Half Retail Value
items.summary: Resale-market analysis finds heavily logoed, season-specific Gucci nylon/canvas bags frequently resell for well under half of original retail, while discontinued classic silhouettes like the Jackie hold or exceed retail -- illustrating a bifurcated resale story that is more cautionary than the brand's classic 'holds value' claim.
